11. Incentive disbursement shall be limited to the budgeted outlay in accordance with the Para  
13.10 of guidelines for PLI scheme as amended from time to time. 
 
12. Claims for disbursement of incentive shall be filed by the PLI Applicant within seven months 
from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains. For example, claim for the 
financial year 2025-26 shall be filed before 31st October, 2026. 
 
13. The MoS/PMA will scrutinize the claim and if it is found that the claim is in accordance with 
the PLI Scheme, the incentive will be paid.  While scrutinizing the incentive claim, the 
MoS/PMA shall have the right to verify the information contained with reference to all the 
documents that may be considered relevant including but not limited to certificates issued by 
statutory auditors and cost auditors, any other information, data or returns furnished to various 
Ministries, Departments or other Government agencies, communications or information filed 
with the stock exchanges, if any or any other information as may be considered appropriate.   
 
14. The PLI applicant shall in form, in writing, the MoS/PMA of any change in the shareholding 
pattern leading to a change in the successor-in-interest during the tenure of the PLI Scheme for 
Specialty Steel after updating the same with the Registrar of Companies. 
 
15. In the case of any change in the shareholding pattern of the company or any other changes 
having the effect of leading to a successor-in-interest coming into existence in the place of the 
PLI Applicant signing this MoU, the successor-in-interest may be assessed for eligibility to get 
incentives under the PLI Scheme . In any such assessment , investment made by the PLI 
Applicant signing this MoU shall be taken into account. 
 
  
                             Page 4 of 5 
16. The parameters applicable for the Successor-in-interest will be the same as determined for the 
selected PLI Applicant to whom approval was accorded under the Scheme. The successor-in-
interest shall express its willingness in writing to the competent authority to comply and adhere 
to all the terms and conditions of the MoU signed by the previous applicant  and file a fresh 
application to the competent authority under the PLI Scheme.  
 
17. The application of successor -in-interest shall be examined by the competent authority and 
approval may be accorded.  While granting such approval, the Competent Authority may 
prescribe additional terms and conditions to be complied with by the successor -in-interest. 
After getting the approval, the successor-in-interest shall sign a fresh MoU with the Ministry 
of Steel agreeing to all terms and conditions accepted by the previous applicant  and also 
additional terms and conditions, if any, specified by the Competent Authority.  
 
18. If the MoS/ PMA, at any stage, finds or comes to know and is satisfied that eligibility  under 
the Scheme and / or disbursement of  incentives  have been obtained by manipulation or 
misrepresentation   or  by furnishing false information,  the MoS/PMA may direct the PLI 
Applicant or its successors or assigns to refund the incentives along with interest calculated  at 
twice the rate of  “3 years SBI  MCLR interest rate’ prevailing  on the  date  of disbursement,  
compounded   annually,  in accordance with the PLI Scheme as amended from time to time, 
after  giving  an opportunity  to the PLI Applicant  of being  heard by the competent authority. 
MoS/PMA may also resort to any other remedy against the selected PLI Applicant  or its 
successors or assigns, as the case may be, in accordance with the PLI Scheme as amended from 
time to time.     
 
19. MoS reserves the right to issue addendum containing further instructions / changes with regard 
to various provisions, to be notified as part of guidelines for PLI scheme.  
 
20. MoS shall be empowered to conduct a financial, compliance and performance audit of the 
beneficiary for claiming incentive under the scheme  and such audit may be conducted by a 
practising  chartered accountant appointed by the MoS. The statutory audit shall be conducted 
by CAG. 
 
21. After signing this MoU, the parties involved shall develop a protocol for  communication 
among them . The communication protocol shall, amongst others, designate the Parties’ 
Coordinators for any and all activities to be performed under this MoU.  
 
22. Neither Party shall be liable (whether based on contract, warranty, indemnity, tort (including 
negligence), strict liability or otherwise) to the other Party for loss of opportunity or profit, 
including loss of the Project, or any special, indirect, exemplary, consequential or incidental 
damages. 
 
23. In case of any dispute relating to  or arising out of this MoU or in connection with any action 
taken or omitted to be taken by either party pursuant to this MoU, the same shall be resolved 
amicably between the parties. In case, the dispute is not amicably settled between the parties, 
the decision of the MoS shall be final. 
 
24. A Performance security of 0.5 % of the committed investment shall be submitted along with 
signing of MoU for each applied product subcategory . The performance security has to be 
valid throughout the PLI scheme period. 
 
25. In case, the company is unable to meet its committed production, thereby not able to claim for 
incentive, for two consecutive years during the scheme period, the performance security (0.5 
% of the committed investment)  may be invoked subject to approval by the competent 
authority.

Updated Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) on  
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Speciality Steel 
1. What is PLI Scheme for specialty steel?
PLI stands for “Production Linked incentive”. The PLI scheme for speciality steel has
been notified by Ministry of Steel, Govt. of India vide Gazette notification
no.F.No.S -21018/1/2020 -TRADE -TAX-PART(1) dated 29 th July, 2021 . Further
amendments are issued vide corrigendum dated 24 th December, 2021.
The objectives of the PLI scheme for specialty steel is to promote manufacturing of
special  steel grades within the country and help the Indian steel industry mature in
terms of technology as well as move up the value chain.
2. What is covered under specialty steel as per the PLI Scheme document?
(Ref. Para 5 of PLI Scheme document and Annexure ‘I’ of the PLI Guidelines)
The scheme covering Special ty steel grades shall be applicable to the following five
(05) indicative broad product categories :
i. Coated/Plated Steel Products
ii. High Strength/ Wear resistant Steel
iii. Specialty Rails – Asymmetric, Head hardened
iv. Alloy Steel Products and Steel wires
v. Electrical Steel – CRGO, CRNO
These are further sub-divided into various product sub-categories. For further details Refer 
Annexure ‘I’  of the PLI Guidelines .

4. What is the tenure of the scheme?
(Ref. sl.no. ii of Corrigendum to the PLI Scheme dated 24th December, 2021 and 
 revised para 3.1 of Modification to the Guidelines dated 24th December, 2021) 
 Incentive under the scheme shall be provided for a maximum period of five (5) years. The 
period of five (5) years shall commence from FY 2023-24 (incentive to be released from FY 
2024-25). The initial year may, however, be deferred by up to two (02) years in case of 
specific product categories within the overall budgetary allocation. The release of incentive 
will be from FY 2024-25 to 2028-29 (FY 2026-27 to 2030-31, in case of deferment by two 
years) based on the achievements made by different companies in keeping with the details

5. Can the scheme period be deferred?
(Revised para 3.2 of Modification to the Guidelines dated 24th December, 2021) 
If considered necessary, due to any special/adverse circumstances (Force majeure condition), 
the selected company(ies) may be allowed to avail the incentive within an extended period 
of up to one (01) year by allowing deferment of the initial year (FY2023-24) with PLI payable 
in FY2024-25 by one year i.e. to FY2024-25 (with PLI payable in FY 2025-26) but the overall 
period for availing incentive being limited to a maximum Period of five (05) years with the 
approval of Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS). 
6. Who can apply under the Scheme?
(Ref. Para 6 of PLI Scheme and Para 2.2 of the Guidelines) 
Individual company/group companies  or JV registered in India under the Companies Act 
2013, desirous of manufacturing identified specialty steel grades, shall be eligible to apply for 
incentive under the scheme. The company shall ensure end-to-end manufacturing of applied 
product sub-category domestically, where the input material is melted and poured within the 
country using iron ore/scrap/sponge iron/pellets, etc. Third party outsourcing is allowed upto 
20% value addition. However, an applicant must meet the criteria as specified in the scheme 
guidelines. 
3 | P a g e 1 6
7. What are target segments of specialty steel products covered under PLI Scheme?
(Ref. Annexure I of the Guidelines) 
Target segments of specialty steel covered under the scheme are one and the same as 
specialty steel segments mentioned in question 2 above. Please refer para 1.4 of the PLI 
Guidelines also. 
8. What is the mode of applying under the PLI scheme?
A company may apply for availing benefits under the scheme through an online portal 
dedicated for the purpose. URL of online portal is :
plimos.mecon.co.in
However, after online submission, the applicant is required to submit the signed copy of the 
submitted application along with all the uploaded documents within 10 days from date of 
online submission and latest by Sept. 25, 2022.
9. Is there any application fee?
Yes, A non-refundable and one-time application fees of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST) for 
each application needs to be paid as per the prescribed mode of payment, without which no 
application shall be evaluated.

11. Can application be submitted through offline mode?
All applications shall be submitted along with uploading of the supporting documents (Refer
Annexure-II of PLI Guidelines ) through an online portal to the Project Management
Agency (PMA).
A physical copy of the submitted application duly signed by the authorized signatory along
with all supporting documents needs to be submitted at the address, (which will be notified
separately), within 10 days of the submission of on-line form.
12. Is applicant required to submit the signed copy of the application submitted online?
Yes. As explained in answer to question No. 11.
13. What are important dates related to application submission?
Important dates related to application submission are as follows.
- Start of application submission for all applicants : 29.12.2021 
- Application submission deadline for all applicants : 15.09.2022 
- Opening of Correction window for all applicants : To be announced 
- Correction window deadline for all applicants : To be announced

The Scheme will be implemented through a Nodal Agency referred as Project
Management Agency (PMA) which will be responsible for providing secretarial,
managerial and implementation support and carrying out other responsibilities as
assigned by Ministry of St eel from time to time.  M/s MECON Limited has been
appointed as PMA by Ministry of Steel.

17. What is application window i.e.  the period for making an  application under the
Scheme?
Online Registration and Application Submission dates are given below.

End Date :- Sept. 15, 2022 
No application shall be received after expiration of the end date. 
18. What is correction window?
A onetime correction window of fifteen days (15 days) after close of application submission
shall be available for completeness of supporting documents only, uploaded along with the
main application form. However, no change in the main application form will be permitted.
19. Can applicant modify the main application after submission?
No
20. Can a company apply for multiple product categories/ sub-categories?
(Ref. Para 8.5 of the PLI Scheme) 
Yes, a company may apply for as many products category/ sub -category as it desires. 
However, it needs to submit separate application for each product sub -category along with 
non-refundable application fee.  
21. What are the eligibility criteria under PLI Scheme?
(Ref. Para 6 of the scheme and para 4 of the Guidelines) 
 The major eligibility criteria are as follows:  
a. The net worth of the company (including that of the group companies)/JV shall not be
less than 30% of the total committed investment. The audited net worth of the company
as reported in the immediate preceding financial year of the date of application
submission shall be considered.
i. In case of new company/group companies/JV incorporated after 31/03/2021 i.e. in
FY22, net worth for such company(ies) shall not be less than 30% of the total committed
investment prior to the date of filing of application.
ii. In case applicant applies in multiple product sub -categories under target
segments, then net worth criteria shall be checked with respect to total
committed investment across all applied product sub-categories.
6 | P a g e 1 6
b. It would be permissible to undertake a maximum 20% of the total value-addition through third
parties (outside the group companies or JVs)
c. Annual incremental production rate: Each applicant shall submit annual incremental
production rate (%) year-wise along with production quantity over the scheme period against
each applied product sub-category. The committed annual incremental production rate must be
equal to or more than the respective threshold incremental produc tion rate mentioned in
Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines for each product sub-category.
d. Unit capacity : Each applicant shall submit unit capacity to be installed against each applied
product sub-category. Each applicant needs to commit minimum unit capacity as per Annexure-
III of PLI Guidelines.
e. Committed investment:  Each applicant shall commit investment against each applied
product sub -category during PLI Scheme period after the date of gazette notification
along with year -wise phasing of investment. The committed investment by applicant
against a particular sub -category has to be equal or more than the minimum unit
investment specified in Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines.
• In case an applicant applies for more than one unit (say, 2 units or 3 units, etc.)
at the same location for simultaneous installation, the followi ng criteria need
to be fulfilled for committed investment
- For simultaneous installation of two units with single combined investment,
the committed investment by the applicant has to be equal or more than the
1.8 times of unit investment specified in the scheme document.
- For simultaneous installation of three units with single combined
investment, the committed investment by the applicant has to be equal or
more than the 2.5 times of unit investment specified in the scheme
document.
• In case an applicant applies for more than one unit (say, 2 units or 3 units, etc.)
at different locations, each unit shall be evaluated independently.
22. Is an applicant having existing unit producing specified product in the scheme eligible
for PLI scheme under that product sub-category ?
Since the unit has become functional as a result of investment made prior to notification of 
the scheme, such applicant shall not be eligible. 
23. What is threshold incremental production rate ?
It is the incremental production rate fixed by MoS for a given product sub - category and 
provided in Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines. Applicants are required to meet or exceed the 
threshold rate while committing annual incremental production rate to be elig ible for 
participation in PLI scheme.     
7 | P a g e 1 6
24. What is Committed rate of annual incremental production (%) year-wise over the entire
scheme period ?
Each applicant shall submit annual incremental production rate (%) year -wise along with 
production quantity over the scheme period against each applied product sub-category. The 
committed annual incremental production rate must be equal to or more than the 
respective threshold incremental production rate mentioned in Annexure-III of PLI 
Guidelines for each product sub-category. 
25. What Committed unit capacity  is to be installed against each applied product sub -
category?
A minimum unit capacity against installation of facilities under each product sub -category
has been defined in Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines. While submitting application, a n
applicant must commit installing a unit having rated capacity ≥  the minimum unit capacity
of its selected product sub-category.
26. What is Committed investment for a given product category/ sub-category along with
year-wise phasing?
A minimum investment against installation of facilities under each product sub-category has
been defined in Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines. An applicant must commit an investment ≥
the minimum investment given against its selected product sub -category. In addition to
above, the year-wise phasing of the total investment committed unde r the scheme duration
needs to be furnished.
27. What are permissible investments?
(Ref. Annexure-IV of PLI Guidelines)
Refer “Annexure-IV of PLI Guidelines” for permissible investments under the PLI scheme.
28. Are investments made before the date of notification of the scheme eligible under
permissible investment?
No. Only the permissible investments done on or after the date of scheme notification i.e.
July 29, 2021 are eligible under the PLI scheme.
29. What is selection process under the Scheme?
(Ref. Para 7 of PLI Guidelines)
Please refer Para 7 of the PLI Guidelines.
30. How are the rankings of applicants determined?
The eligible applicants shall be ranked based on the following evaluation criteria
8 | P a g e 1 6  
 
a) Committed annual incremental production during the scheme period - 50 % weightage 
b) Committed investment as per the list of permissible investments – 50 % weightage. 
31. What is evaluation criteria in case of tie in ranking between/ among the applicants for 
any given product category/ sub-category? 
 
In case where the same rank has been secured by the eligible companies, the final 
selection shall be based on the following rules (in the following descending order)  
i. An eligible company committing to upfront investments shall be given 
priority as indicated in the PLI Scheme document.  
ii. Eligible company who has applied first shall be given priority over others, all 
else being equal. 
 
32. How is incentive calculated under PLI scheme? 
        
The incentive shall be calculated as per the formula given below : 
Incentive = (A/B) x (B or C or D, whichever is lowest) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100 
A = Incremental sales in current year # 
B = Weighted Average sales price of the applicant in current year 
C = Weighted Average sales price in base year (Refer Annexure – V of PLI 
Guidelines) 
D = Weighted Average sale price in current year $ 
# Current year : means year for which PLI has been claimed 
$ To be worked out by JPC and notified by PMA every year 
 
However, a detailed  methodology of incentive calculation shall be notified separately. 
33. What are eligibility criteria for claiming the PLI? 
 
 Please refer Para 11& 13 of  PLI Guidelines. 
 
34. What is the Limiting investment? 
 
The concept shall be applicable only to qualify at the time of incentive calculation. 
However, at the time of application, committed investment must equal or exceed unit 
investment.  
It is defined as the following for a particular sub-category: 
Case-1: Applicant has applied for single unit for a particular product sub-category 
80% of committed investment, or unit investment as given in Annexure-III of PLI 
Guidelines, whichever is lower 
Case-2: Applicant has applied for two simultaneous units, at same location, for a 
particular product sub-category 
80% of committed investment, or 1.8 x unit investment as given in the Annexure-
III of PLI Guidelines, whichever is lower 
9 | P a g e 1 6
Case-3: Applicant has applie d for three simultaneous units, at same location, for a 
particular product sub-category 
80% of committed investment, or 2.5 x unit investment as given in the Annexure-
III of PLI Guidelines, whichever is lower 
35. What is the Limiting incremental production rate?
It is defined as the following:
80% of the committed annual incremental production rate or Threshold annual
incremental production rate as given in Annexure-III of PLI Guidelines, whichever is
higher.
36. What if an applicant company is not able to meet threshold incremental production rate
for a given year?
For incremental production eligible for incentive in a year, the actual incremental production
rate shall be equal to or more than the Limiting Incremental Production rate in that year.
In case an applicant company is not able to meet Limiting Incremental Production rate, then
no incentive shall be payable to the company for that year.
37. What if an applicant company is not able to meet committed investment?
In case the actual permissible investment made by each selected company as per
Annexure-IV of PLI Guidelines, is less than the limiting investment for a product sub -
category, the selected company shall not be eligible for any incentive.
38. Are taxes and duties included in the expenditure that is considered towards
Investment in the Scheme?
All non-creditable taxes and duties would be included in such expenditure.
39. Is the expenditure incurred on Land and Building covered under the Scheme?
 Yes. 
40. Can a company availing incentive under PLI scheme also claim incentive under other
schemes of Govt. of India or State Governments?
(Ref. Para 6.7 & 6.8 of PLI Scheme)
i. An eligible company availing benefit under the PLI scheme of Ministry of Steel may avail
benefit under other scheme such as the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported products
(RoDTEP) or those of the State Governments.
ii. An eligible company availing benefit under the PLI scheme of Ministry of Steel is not barred
from availing benefits under PLI schemes of other Ministries / Departments for products
other than those identified under the PLI Scheme for ‘Specialty Steel’. However, the eligible
investments / sales considered for benefits under this scheme shall not be considered for
fiscal benefits under PLI schemes of other Ministries / Departments.
10 | P a g e 1 6  
 
41. What percentage of used/refurbished plant, machinery and equipment is considered as 
Investment under the Scheme and what  are the other factors  considered while  
importing or  domestically procuring used / refurbished plant, machinery and 
equipment? 
 
(Ref. Annexure-IV of PLI Guidelines) 
Used/refurbished plant, machinery and equipment are not permitted  towards Investment 
under the Scheme. 
 
42. Is the cost for R&D during the investment period included for the purpose  of 
Investment under PLI Scheme? 
 
(Ref. Annexure-IV of PLI Guidelines) 
Yes 
 
43.  Is there any annual ceiling on the incentive payable to each applicant?  
 
(Ref. Para 8.4 of PLI Scheme) 
The annual incentive payable shall be capped at ₹200 crore per eligible company 
(including that of group companies/JV) across all categories or sub -categories  of the 
target product segments . 
44. When can an applicant submit a claim for disbursements under PLI? 
        
Claims for disbursement of incentive shall be filed by the Selected companies within 7 
months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains. For example, Claim 
for say, FY2 4-25 has to be submitted within 31st October, 202 5. 
45. What are the mandatory documents that need to be submitted along with  the 
information submitted in the application form? 
 
(Ref. Annexure-II of PLI Guidelines) 
The Applicant needs to upload the mandatory documents in support of the information 
submitted in the application form as detailed in Annexure-II of PLI Guidelines. 
46. What is EGoS? 
 
An Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS) shall be constituted to monitor the PLI scheme. 
The EGoS shall be headed by the Cabinet Secretary. 
47. Who is Competent Authority? 
The Competent Authority shall be the Minister of Steel or as delegated by him. 
 
48.  Is there any requirement of submitting performance security? What is the underlying 
rationale for its submission? How much performance security is to be submitted? 
 
Yes, following selection of the applicant for a particular product sub -category, selected 
company has to submit a performance security @ 0.5% of the committed investment, against 
11 | P a g e 1 6  
 
the unit capacity for that product sub -category, as notified in the scheme do cument or 
guidelines, along with the memorandum of understanding . The performance security is 
desired so that applicant while submitting application and to secure higher ranking, may not 
resort to giving unrealistic commitment on investment, capacity and y ear-wise incremental 
rate of production.  The performance security has to be valid throughout the PLI scheme 
period. 
49. Will the performance security be separate for each product sub-category?  
 
Yes. A company applying for say, 3 product sub-categories, and if selected in each of them, 
shall have to submit three (3) different performance security @0.5% of the committed 
investment against the unit capacity in each of the three product sub-categories, along with 
the respective Memorandum of Understanding.      
50. Can the performance security be encashed?  
 
Yes. In case, the company is unable to meet its committed production, ther eby not 
able to claim for incentive, for the two consecutive years during the scheme period, 
his performance security may be en -cashed subject to approval by the competent 
authority. 
51. What is the basis of computation of incentive ?  
 
Computation of the incentive shall be done as per the provisions of the scheme as detailed 
in the guideline under para 12 and 13.  
 
52. What is weighted average sale price ?  
 
The weighted average sale price in a particular financial year for a particular product sub-
category shall be determined in the following manner : 
 
If Q1, Q2, Q3 -----------------up to say, Q10 quantities  (in tonnes) of above product sub -
category in a financial year are sold at P1, P2, P3------------------ up to say, P10 sales prices 
(in Rs.) , then weighted average sale price = (Q1.P1+Q2.P2+Q3.P3+........Q10.P10)  / 
(Q1+Q2+Q3+..........Q10) in Rs./tonne. 
 
53. What are the mechanisms for resolution of complaints/grievances arising during the 
tenure of the scheme ?  
 
Ministry of Steel (MoS) shall constitute and notify a ” Grievance Redressal 
Committee” to oversee the complaints/ grievances arising out of the PLI s cheme at 
any stage of the scheme. 
 
Note:- Annexure –III & Annexure -IV of Guideline shall be read along with 
Modification to Guidelines issued on December 24th 2021. 
12 | P a g e 1 6
54 :  Whether production from existing units is eligible for claiming incentive? 
No, Production from the new unit installed under PLI Scheme shall only be considered for 
claiming incentive. 
55 :  How production target shall be calculated for each year of the scheme for applicants 
having existing baseline production? 
As per clause 11.3 of the guidelines, minimum threshold production for becoming eligible 
is elaborated in following manner.  
Minimum production target from the new unit, in the first year of scheme, shall be the 
incremental production arrived at by applying minimum year -on-year incremental 
production rate as notified in the scheme, over existing baseline production in the base 
year or previous year, whichever is higher.  
In the subsequent years, production shall be calculated by applying minimum year-on-year 
incremental productio n rate over the committed production or production target in the 
previous year, whichever is higher, calculated by the PMA as per the scheme. 
Applicants shall submit the rationale and justification for the commitment of production at 
the end of the scheme based on industry practices of capacity utilization. 
56 :  How production target shall be calculated for each year of the scheme for applicants 
having nil baseline production in the applied product sub-category 
Production target from the new unit, in the first year of scheme, shall be arrived by working 
backwards from the production target if at the end of the scheme year based on notified 
CAGR for that product sub-category. 
In the subsequent years, production shall be calculated by applying minimum year-on-year 
incremental production rate over the committed production or production target whichever 
is higher in the previous year, calculated by the PMA as per the scheme. 
Applicants shall submit the rationale and justification for the production target at the end of 
the scheme based on industry practices of capacity utilization. 
57:  How threshold shall apply on commitment of rated production prior to final year of 
the Scheme ? 
An applicant committing to achieve the rated capacity of the installed unit prior to the end 
of the final year of the scheme shall be eligible for claiming incentives by participating in 
the scheme as per provisions of the scheme. Once rated production capacity is achieved, 
no incentive shall be disbursed. 
58:  What is minimum unit capacity and minimum investment for eligibility in case of sub-
category 3(a) & 3(b)? 
For sub-categories 3(a) & 3(b), if same unit is producing both sub -categories of products 
–cumulative minimum unit capacity (250,000 t/yr) and single minimum investment (Rs 200
cr) shall be applicable. Otherwise, unit capacity (250,000 t/yr) and minimum investment
(Rs 200 Cr) shall be distinct for 3(a) & 3(b). However, the selected company has  to enter
MoU with Ministry of Steel separately for each sub-category.
13 | P a g e 1 6  
 
59:  How many applicants shall be selected in respective product sub-category? 
It will be guided by provisions of the clause 7.7 of the guideline. However, Ministry of Steel 
shall take final decision for number of selected applicants in any product sub -category 
based on the ranking.   
60.    Whether para 16.7 and 16.8 omitted deliberately or typographical error? 
Yes, there is one typographical error in the notified guidelines.  
Errata: 
The following clauses shall be added to para 16 of guidelines by Ministry of Steel, after 
para 16.6 and before 16.9:

each applied product s ub-category. For example, if an applicant applies and is 
selected for 3 product sub -categories, the company  has to submit 3 different 
performance security @0.5% of the committed investment against each case. The 
performance security has to be valid throughout the PLI scheme period. In case, the 
company is unable to meet its committed production, thereby not able to claim for 
incentive, for two consecutive years during the scheme period, the performance

16.8. For promotion of transparency and equity, keeping in view the various instructions of 
the Central   Vigilance Commission   regarding adoption of an Integrity Pact in the 
matter of procurement, it has been decided to obtain undertaking(s) from applicants 
under the Scheme. In this regard, the format of undertakings as given in Annexure-
VIII of the guidelines needs to be furnished by the selected companies. 
61: In case a single investment is made in single manufacturing unit to produce multiple 
sub-category products under PLI, whether one may applicant apply in multiple sub-
categories? 
Applicants applying in multiple sub-categories but proposing to use the same unit should 
comply with the individual and cumulative thresholds independently. 
Separate MoUs shall be signed by the companies for each product sub -category the 
company intends to participate.

Additional Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) on  
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Speciality Steel  
– Specific to PLI Scheme 1.2 
 
1 | P a g e 7  
 
1.  What are various rounds of PLI Scheme for Specialty Steel viz. PLI Scheme 1.0, PLI 
Scheme 1.1, and PLI Scheme 1.2 ? 
PLI Scheme  for specialty steel was  notified vide gazette notification on 29 th July, 
2021 , Application  window closed on Sept. 15, 2022 and  MoUs were signed on 17 
March 2023.  Subsequently, when 2 nd round of the scheme was launched in 2025, 
this round was termed as PLI scheme was termed as PLI Scheme 1.0.  
Second round of PLI Scheme for specialty steel termed as PLI Scheme 1.1 was 
launched on 06 th January 2025, application  was closed on 17th February 2025  and 
MoUs were signed on 24 th March 202 5. 
Third round of PLI Scheme for specialty steel termed as PLI Scheme 1.2  has been 
launched  on 04 th November 2025 .  
2. Where can one find details about the schemes  
One can access dedicated web portal  (https://plimos.mecon.co.in 
https://plimos.mecon.co.in/) for all the details such as scheme documents, guidelines, 
registration details as applicant, and latest notifications.  
3. Which product categories are covered under PLI Scheme 1.2? 
The are 22 Product sub -categories under  4 broad categories and detailed list can be 
found  in Annexure ‘I’  of the PLI Scheme 1.2 Guidelines.  The guidelines can be 
downloaded from web portal.

5. Who needs to give end user certificate? 
Applicants under the product sub-category 1 to 5 under product category “Steel grades for 
strategic sector” needs to provide the end user certificate for claiming incentive against 
production quantities derived from sales to be end used in strategic sector only.  
 
6. Where from the applicants need to obtain the end user certificate? 
Applicants under the product sub-category 1 to 5 under product category “Steel grades for 
strategic sector” needs to obtain the end user certificate for use in strategic sector from their 
customers whom they have sold the products under applied product sub-category of the 
scheme. 
7. Does the end user have to be direct customer? 
The end user may or may not be the direct customer. But chain of end use needs to be 
clearly established in the end user certificate. 
8. Is export quantities eligible for incentive claim ? 
Yes, for all product sub-categories. 
9. What is incentive outlay under the scheme? 
The total budget outlay of the PLI Scheme for specialty steel is Rs. 6322 Crore. 
10. What is the tenure of the scheme? 
Tenure for Production year Tenure for Incentive disbursement year 
FY 2025 – 26 to FY 2029 – 30  FY 2026 – 27 to FY 2030 – 31  
 
11. What is the mode of applying under the PLI scheme 1.2? 
A company may apply under the scheme through an online portal for the purpose. 
URL of online portal is: https://plimos.mecon.co.in

12. Is there any application fee? 
Yes, A non-refundable and one -time application fees of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST) 
for each application needs to be paid as per the prescribed mode of payment, without which 
no application shall be evaluated.

14. Can application be submitted through offline mode? 
No, All applications are required to  be submitted through online mode only through PLI 
web portal - https://plimos.mecon.co.in.  
A hard copy of the submitted online application duly signed by the authorized signatory 
along with all supporting documents needs to be submitted at the address  given below , 
within 10 days of end date of application submission. 
The Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills,  
& Nodal Officer PLI Scheme for Specialty steel, 
MECON Limited, Vivekanand Path, Doranda, Ranchi-834002

   Yes. As explained above. 
16. Who is PMA and what is its role? 
The Scheme will be implemented through a Nodal Agency referred as Project   
Management Agency (PMA) which will be responsible for providing secretarial, 
managerial and implementation support and carrying out other responsibilities as assigned

18. What is correction window? 
A onetime correction window of fifteen days (15 days) shall be notified on the PLI Portal 
after close of application submission and evaluation by PMA. It will  be available for 
completeness of supporting documents only  that were  uploaded along with the main 
application. However, no change in the main application form will be permitted. 
19. Can applicant modify the main application after submission? 
No 
20. Can a company apply for multiple product categories/ sub-categories? 
Yes, a company may apply for as many products category/ sub -category as it desires. 
However, it needs to submit separate application for each product sub -category along with 
non-refundable application fee. 
21. What is the eligibility criteria for applying under PLI Scheme 1.2? 
Please  refer to clause 4 of PLI Scheme guidelines 1.2. 
22. What is threshold incremental production rate? 
Not applicable for PLI Scheme 1.2. 
23. What is Committed rate of annual incremental production (%) year -wise over the 
entire scheme period? 
Not applicable for PLI Scheme 1.2. 
24. What Committed unit capacity  is to be installed against each applied product sub -
category?  
Refer to Annexure-I of PLI scheme 1.2 guidelines.

26. Are investments made before the date of notification of the scheme eligible under 
permissible investment? 
No. Only the permissible investments done on or after 4th November, 2025 are admissible 
under the PLI Scheme 1.2. 
27. How is incentive calculated under PLI scheme?  
Please refer to clause 8 of the PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
28. What if an applicant company is not able to meet committed investment? 
In case the actual permissible investment made by each selected company as per Annexure-
I of PLI scheme 1.2 Guidelines, is less than the limiting investment for a product sub -
category, the selected company shall not be eligible for claiming incentive. 
29. Are taxes and duties included in the expenditure that is considered towards 
Investment in the Scheme? 
All non-creditable taxes and duties would be included in such expenditure. 
30. Is the expenditure incurred on Land and Building covered under the Scheme? 
Yes. 
31. Is there any annual ceiling on the incentive payable to each applicant?  
Refer Clause 9.1 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
32. When can an applicant submit a claim for disbursements under PLI? 
Refer Clause 9.4 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
33. What are the mandatory documents that need to be submitted along with  the 
information submitted in the application form? 
Refer appendix-A of Annexure-II of PLI scheme 1.2 Guidelines. 
34.  Is there any requirement of submitting performance security ? What is the underlying 
rationale for its submission? How much performance security is to be submitted? 
Yes, Refer Clause 5.3 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
35. Will the performance security be separate for each product sub-category?  
Yes.

37.  where is clause 9.10 
There is one typographical error in the clause 9.8 wherein clause 9.10 is referred. Please 
read it as 9.9 under this clause. 
38. In case a single investment is made in single manufacturing unit to produce multiple 
sub-category products under PLI, whether one may applicant apply in multiple sub-
categories? 
Applicants applying in multiple sub -categories but proposing to use the same unit should 
comply with the individual and cumulative thresholds independently. 
Separate MoUs shall be signed by the companies for each product sub -category the 
company intends to participate.

Communication from Ministry of Steel on approval of modification of PLI Scheme 
guidelines  to be applicable for future rounds of application  
 
i. Allowing companies to commit to 50% threshold investment if they propose to create 
capacity through augmentation of existing capacities. 
ii. Reducing the thresholds for participation in high value Cold Rolled Grain-Oriented 
(CRGO) with investment threshold reduction from Rs.5000 crore to Rs.3000 crore, 
capacity threshold reduction from 200,000 tonnes to 50,000 tonnes and year-on-year 
growth threshold reduction from 40% to 10%. 
iii. Allowing carry forward of excess production to the immediately following year for the 
purpose of claiming incentive.”

PLI Scheme 1.1
Eligibility to participate in PLI Scheme 1.1
• Steel manufacturers engaged in end-to-end steel manufacturing in India 
• Companies creating new facility and augmenting existing facilities can apply
• The companies participating in PLI scheme 1.0 scheme shall be eligible to apply for  
the same product sub-category or different product sub-categories under PLI 
scheme 1.1. However, the project(s) of any company under the PLI scheme 1.0 for 
which MoU has been signed shall not qualify to participate in the PLI scheme 1.1
• Commitments to investment and incremental production necessary as per threshold
• 50% of threshold investment allowed in case of augmentation

Clause  no 2.13 : Commissioning Year / Month: refers to 
the commencement of production from the unit in a 
particular year / month. Commissioning year and year of 
commencement of production are one and same and 
have been used interchangeably. 
The date of commencement of production shall refer to 
the date on which the applicant raises the first GST invoice 
for the sale of eligible product(s) manufactured under the 
scheme
Commissioning Year / Month: refers to the commencement of 
production from the unit in a particular year / month. 
Commissioning year and year of commencement of production 
are one and same and have been used interchangeably.
In case of Capacity addition The date of commencement of 
production shall refer to the date on which the applicant raises 
the first GST invoice for the sale of eligible product(s) 
manufactured under the scheme. 
In case of Capacity augmentation The first year of commitment 
of production as per application.
Clause No- 2.38 :Unit capacity
refers to rated capacity of the plant to produce eligible 
product(s) of a given product sub-category. 
Unit capacity :refers to rated capacity of the plant to produce 
eligible product(s) of a given product sub-category. 
In case of  Capacity addition: It is the newly installed capacity.
In case of Capacity augmentation: It is the capacity after 
augmentation.
Clause No- 4.1
The company (applicant) shall make an investment in 
creating new capacity for manufacturing of the applied 
sub-category product.
The company (applicant) shall make an investment to enhance 
the capacity either by capacity addition or by capacity 
augmentation  for manufacturing of the applied sub-category 
product
January 06, 2025
 
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
         
PLI Scheme 1.1
Modification in clauses of existing Guidelines for PLI 1.1
Clauses in PLI 1.0 Modified for PLI 1.1 
4.2.4.1 : Annual incremental 
production rate: Each applicant 
shall submit annual incremental 
production rate (%) year-wise 
along with production quantity 
over the scheme period against 
each applied product sub-
category. The committed annual 
incremental production rate 
must be equal to or more than 
the respective threshold 
incremental production rate 
mentioned in Annexure-III for 
each product sub-category. 
Annual incremental production rate: Each applicant shall submit annual incremental 
production rate (%) year-wise along with production quantity over the scheme period 
against each applied product sub-category. The committed annual incremental production 
rate must be equal to or more than the respective threshold incremental production rate 
mentioned in Annexure-III for each product sub-category. The annual incremental
production rate shall be applicable on the committed production by applicants for each
year of the scheme tenure except first year. 
The criteria for commitment for the first year shall be as follows. - For capacity addition,
It shall be any production quantity as committed by applicant. However, in case of capacity
augmentation, the production commitment shall be more than the highest of the
production in last 3 preceding financial years i.e. FY 2021-22, FY 2022-23 & FY 2023-24 of
the applied product sub-category.
Annual Incremental production rate shall be applicable till rated capacity is achieved. 
Clause No- 11.1  & 14.3.2
 Committed investment is a 
condition to be fulfilled prior to 
commencement of production 
and claiming incentive
Limiting investment is a condition to be fulfilled prior claiming incentive
Thank You

असाधारण  
EXTRAORDINARY 
भाग I—खण् ड 1  
PART I—Section 1 
प्राजधकार से प्रकाजित 
PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY 
 
 
इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय 
अजधसूचना  
नई दिल्ली, 29  िुलाई, 2021 
जिषय :  भारत में जििेष इस्ट्पात क े जलए उत्पािन-संबद्ध प्रोत्साहन योिना (पीएलआई) 
फा.सं. एस-21018/1/2020-रेड -टैक्स-भाग (1).— 
1. पृष्ठभूजम 
मंजत्रमंडल ने भारत में जििेष इस्ट्पात क े जलए 6,322 करोड़ रूपए क े  पररव्यय क े  साथ जित्त िषष 2023-24 से 2029-
30 तक कायाषजवित की िाने िाली उत्पािन-संबद्ध प्रोत्साहन योिना (पीएलआई) योिना को अनुमोिन प्रिान कर 
दिया है। 
2. उद्देश्य 
जििेष ग्रेड इस्ट्पात क े  जलए पीएलआई योिना का उद्देश्य िेि में ऐसे इस्ट्पात ग्रेड क े  जिजनमाषण को बढािा िेना है। 
ितषमान में, भारत इस्ट्पात जिजनमाषण की मूल्य श्ृंख्ला क े  जनचले स्ट्तर पर संचालन करता है, िहााँ प्रजत टन औसत 
प्राजि क े िल ₹ 51,000 - ₹ 58,000 है, इसक े  जिपरीत हमारे इस्ट्पात आयात का औसत मूल्य प्रजत टन लगभग ₹ 
1,46,000-₹ 1,83,000 है। पीएलआई प्रोत्साहन ‘जििेष इस्ट्पात’ क े  घरेलू उत्पािन को बढािा िेगा और िेि में 
‘जििेष इस्ट्पात’ क े  उत्पािन हेतु महत्िपूणष जनिेि को आकर्षषत करेगा। यह भारतीय इस्ट्पात उद्योग को प्रौद्योजगकी क े  
मामले में पररपक्व होने क े  साथ-साथ मूल्य श्ृंखला में आगे बढने में मिि करेगा। 
सं.    209] नई दिल्ली, बृहस्ट् पजतिार, िुलाई 29,  2021/श्ािण 7, 1943  
No.  209] NEW DELHI,  THURSDAY, JULY 29, 2021/SHRAVANA 7, 1943  
सी.जी.-डी.एल.-अ.-29072021-228562
CG-DL-E-29072021-228562
2  THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY    [PART I—SEC.1] 
3. प्रोत्साहन की मात्रा 
यह योिना पात्र क ं पजनयों को ताजलका-1 में िी गई िरों पर उत्पािन-संबद्ध प्रोत्साहन प्रिान करेगी। 
ताजलका 1 
पीएलआई स्ट्लैब 2022-23 2023-24 2024-25 2025-26 2026-27 
पीएलआई – क 4% 5% 5% 4% 3% 
पीएलआई – ख 8% 9% 10% 9% 7% 
पीएलआई – ग 12% 15% 15% 13% 11% 
 
4. प्रोत्साहन स्ट्लैब 
प्रोत्साहन स्ट्लैब को ितषमान उत्पािन क े  आधार पर प्रस्ट्ताजित दकया गया है, याजन उन जििेष ग्रेडों क े जलए उच्चतर 
प्रोत्साहन है, िो ितषमान में या तो भारत में उत्पादित नहीं होते हैं या कम मात्रा में उत्पादित होते हैं, जिसक े  
पररणामस्ट्िरूप इनका अपेक्षाक ृत िृहत आयात होता है। इन जििेष इस्ट्पातों क े  औसत मूल्य को ध्यान में रखते हुए, 
इन ग्रेडों क े आयात क े  पररणामस्ट्िरूप िेि से अत्यजधक जििेिी मुद्रा का बजहगषमन होता है।  
5. लक्ष्य सेग्मेंट 
यह योिना जनम्नानुसार सूचीबद्ध पााँच (05) सांक े जतक उत्पाि श्ेजणयों पर लागू होगी: 
5.1.  यह योिना जनम्नानुसार सूचीबद्ध पााँच (05) सांक े जतक उत्पाि श्ेजणयों पर लागू होगी: 
5.1.1. लेजपत/प्लेटेड इस्ट्पात उत्पाि 
5.1.2. उच्च िजि/जघसाि प्रजतरोधी इस्ट्पात 
5.1.3. स्ट्पेजियलटी रेल 
5.1.4. जमश् धातु इस्ट्पात उत्पाि और स्ट्टील िायर 
5.1.5. इलेजक्रकल स्ट्टील 
उपयुषि पााँच उत्पाि श्ेजणयों क े  अंतगषत उत्पाि उप-श्ेजणयों की जनिषिक सूची, पात्रता मापिंडों क े  साथ पीएलआई 
स्ट्लैब का जििरण पररजिष्ट-ग पर संलग्न है। ये सांक े जतक हैं तथा जििरण योिना दििा-जनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया 
िाएगा। यदि आिश्यक समझा गया तो सजचिों क े  अजधकारप्राि समूह (ईिीओएस) क े  अनुमोिन द्वारा इस्ट्पात 
मंत्रालय द्वारा यथा-जनधाषररत अनुसार व्यापक श्ेजणयों, उप-श्ेजणयों, पात्रता मापिंडों, पीएलआई िरों आदि में कोई 
भी पररितषन दकया िा सकता है। 
6. पात्रता 
6.1. क ं पनी अजधजनयम, 2013 क े  तहत भारत में पंिीक ृ त क ं पनी, िो जनधाषररत ‘जििेष इस्ट्पात’ ग्रेड क े  एंड-टू-एंड 
जनमाषण में लगी हुई है, बिते इनपुट सामग्री को लौह अयस्ट्क/स्ट्र ै प/स्ट्पंि आयरन/पैलेट का उपयोग कर िेि में ही 
जपघलाया और ढाला िाता हो, इस योिना क े  तहत प्रोत्साहन क े  जलए आिेिन करने क े  पात्र होंगे। 
6.2. संयुि उद्यम भी इस योिना में भाग लेने हेतु पात्र है। 
6.3. आिेिन की जतजथ क े  अनुसार क ं पनी (समूह की क ं पजनयों सजहत)/िेिी की जनिल संपजत्त क ु ल प्रजतबद्ध जनिेि क े 
30% से कम नहीं होनी चाजहए, िैसा दक दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया गया हो। 
6.4. तीसरे पक्षों (समूह क ं पजनयों या िेिी क े  बाहर) क े  माध्यम से क ु ल मूल्यिधषन क े  अजधकतम 20% का भार उठाने 
की अनुमजत होगी; हालांदक, ऐसे मामलों में प्रोत्साहन का िािा क े िल उसी क ं पनी द्वारा दकया िा सकता है, 
जिसने पीएलआई योिना क े तहत पात्र अंजतम उत्पाि उप-श्ेणी का जिजनमाषण दकया है। 
[भाग I—खण् ड 1] भारत का रािपत्र : असाधारण 3 
6.5. पीएलआई योिना क े  तहत भागीिारी हेतु पात्र होने क े जलए क ं पनी द्वारा प्रजतबद्ध की िाने िाली प्रस्ट्ताजित 
वयूनतम अहषक पात्रता सीमा क े  जििरण को योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा। श्ेणीिार उत्पाि 
प्रत्याजित क ु ल जनिेि और पीएलआई पात्रता क े जलए वयूनतम िृजद्धिील उत्पािन की सांक े जतक सूची अनुलग्नक-ग 
क े  रूप में संलग्न है। 
6.6. यदि आिश्यक समझा िाता है तो दकसी भी पात्रता मानिंड में कोई भी पररितषन इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा सजचिों 
क े  अजधकारप्राि समूह (ईिीओएस) क े  अनुमोिन से दकया िा सकता है। 
6.7. इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय की पीएलआई योिना क े तहत लाभ प्राि करने िाली पात्र क ं पनी अवय योिनाओं क े  तहत लाभ, 
िैसे दक जनयाषत उत्पािों पर िुल्क और करों की छ ू ट (आरओडीटीईपी) या राज्य सरकारों की योिनाओं का लाभ 
उठा सकती है। 
6.8. इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय की पीएलआई योिना क े  तहत लाभ प्राि करने िाली पात्र क ं पनी को ‘जििेष  इस्ट्पात’ क े  जलए 
पीएलआई योिना क े  तहत जनधाषररत उत्पािों क े  अलािा अवय मंत्रालयों/जिभागों की पीएलआई योिनाओं क े 
तहत लाभ प्राि करने पर रोक नहीं है। तथाजप, इस योिना क े तहत लाभ क े  जलए समझे गए पात्र जनिेिों/जबरी 
पर अवय मंत्रालयों/जिभागों की पीएलआई योिनाओं क े अंतगषत जित्तीय लाभ क े  जलए जिचार नहीं दकया िाएगा। 
7. चयन 
7.1. क ं पनी अजधजनयम, 2013 क े तहत भारत में पंिीक ृ त पात्र क ं पजनयों/संयुि उद्यमों (इसक े  बाि पात्र 
क ं पनी/क ं पजनयों क े रूप में संिर्भषत) को िॉटषजलस्ट्ट करने हेतु एक पारििी चयन प्रदरया को योिना क े  दििा-
जनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा। 
7.2. उत्पािन-संबद्ध प्रोत्साहन का िािा करने क े  जलए योिना में भाग लेने की इच्छ ु क पात्र क ं पजनयााँ जनधाषररत प्रारूप 
में यथा-अजधसूजचत आिेिन िुल्क क े  साथ आिेिन करक े  योिना में िाजमल हो सकती हैं। 
7.3. पात्र क ं पजनयों को मानकों पर प्रजतबद्धताएं करने की आिश्यकता होगी, याजन वयूनतम िषष-िर-िषष िृजद्धिील 
उत्पािन और जनिेि, िो फ्र ं ट-लोडेड हो या जहस्ट्सों में दकए गए हों। 
7.4. आिेिनों की िााँच की िाएगी और आिेिक द्वारा प्रस्ट्ताजित पााँच उत्पाि श्ेजणयों या उप-श्ेजणयों में से प्रत्येक में से 
पात्र आिेिकों का चयन दकया िाएगा। 
7.5. पात्र क ं पजनयों में से प्रत्येक उत्पाि-श्ेणी/उप-श्ेणी में जनम्नजलजखत मानिंडों क े  आधार पर चयन दकया िाएगा:- 
7.5.1. वयूनतम िषष -िर-िषष िृजद्धिील उत्पािन प्रजतबद्धता – क ु ल भार 50% 
7.5.2. उस उत्पाि-श्ेणी/उप-श्ेणी क े जलए वयूनतम जनिेि प्रजतबद्धता – क ु ल भार 50%। 7.5.2.1 
प्रत्येक उत्पाि-श्ेणी/उप-श्ेणी में होने िाला अनुमत्य जनिेि योिना दििा-जनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया 
िाएगा। 
7.6. योिना की अजधसूचना की तारीख क े बाि पात्र क ंपजनयों द्वारा दकए गए जनिेि को पीएलआई योिना क े तहत 
‘पात्र जनिेि’ माना िाएगा, बिते दक यह जनिेि संबंजधत उत्पाि श्ेणी/उप-श्ेणी क े  जलए अनुमत्य जनिेि की सूची 
में से हों। इस प्रकार दकए गए जनिेि को योिना अिजध क े  िौरान आिेिकों द्वारा दकए िाने िाले क ु ल जनिेि में 
जगना िाएगा। उत्पािन श्ेणी/उप-श्ेणी िार ‘अनुमत्य जनिेिों की सूची’ योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत की 
िाएगी। 
7.7. अनुमत्य जनिेिों की सूची में योिना दििाजनिेिों में यथा-अजधसूजचत पूाँिीगत अनुसंधान एिं जिकास में जनिेि, 
प्रौद्योजगकी हस्ट्तांतरण पर व्यय, बौजद्धक संपिा अजधकारों क े जलए भुगतान, गुणित्ता जनयंत्रण उपकरण िाजमल 
होंगे। 
7.8. प्रभारी मंत्री क े  अनुमोिन क े बाि, िाटषजलस्ट्ट की गई पात्र क ं पजनयों को, इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा चयजनत 
क ं पजनयों क े  रूप में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा।

7.9. पीएलआई योिना क े  अंतगषत चयजनत क ं पजनयों को चयन क े  समय की गई प्रजतबद्धताओं  का पालन करते हुए 
इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय क े  साथ एक समझौता ज्ञापन पर हस्ट्ताक्षर करना होगा, िो पीएलआई संजितरण क े  अंजतम िषष 
तक िैध होगा। 
7.10. जनष्पािन प्रजतभूजत िो दक प्रजतबद्ध जनिेि क े 0.5% से कम नहीं हो सकती, िैसा दक योिना दििाजनिेि में 
अजधसूजचत दकया िाए, समझौता ज्ञापन क े  साथ प्रस्ट्तुत की िाएगी।  
7.11. योिना दििाजनिेिों में पूिष अहषता एिं चयन मानिंड संबंधी जििरण अलग से अजधसूजचत दकए िाएंगे।  
8. अवय ितें  
8.1. योग्य क ं पजनयों क े  पास फ्र ं ट-लोडडंग जनिेि या अंिों में जनिेि का जिकल्प होगा। यदि संभाजित    जनमाषता क ु छ 
िमनकारी पररजस्ट्थजतयों िैसे दक अप्रत्याजित घटना क े  कारण जनिेि अनुसूची में आस्ट्थगन चाहता है, तो उस पर 
सजचिों क े  अजधकारप्राि समूह (ईिीओएस) क े  अनुमोिन से जिचार दकया िा सकता है। तथाजप, पीएलआई क े 
पूणष संजितरण का िािा करने क े जलए पात्र क ं पजनयों द्वारा पूणष संचयी जनिेि करने की आिश्यकता होगी।  
8.2. िैसी पात्र क ं पजनयों को िरीयता िी िाएगी िो योिना अिजध क े  िौरान अपने जनिेि को फ्र ं ट-लोड करने की 
प्रजतबद्धता िेंगे। जनिेि प्रजतबद्धता का मूल्यांकन बैंक िर को ध्यान में रखते हुए िर पर बट्टागत िुद्ध ितषमान 
मूल्य, िो योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकए िाएं, क े आधार पर दकया िाना प्रस्ट्ताजित है। 
8.3. चूंदक यह योिना सीजमत जनजध िाली होगी, इसजलए यदि क ं पनी दकसी भी दिए गए िषष में प्रजतबद्ध सीमा को 
पूरा करने में जिफल रहती है, तो उसे उस िषष क े  जलए कोई लाभ नहीं जमलेगा। तथाजप, यह अगले िषष इस 
योिना क े तहत लाभ प्राि करने क े जलए पात्र होगी यदि यह उस िषष क े  जलए जनधाषररत संचयी प्रजतबद्ध सीमाओं 
को पूरा करेगी। जनििषक अनुमाजनत िृजद्धिील उत्पािन का जििरण पररजिष्ट-ख में ििाषया गया है। प्रत्येक उत्पाि 
उप-श्ेणी क े  जलए अनुमाजनत िृजद्धिील उत्पािन का जििरण योिना दििा-जनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा। 
8.4. कोई पात्र क ं पनी कई उत्पाि-श्ेजणयों/उप-श्ेजणयों में भाग लेने क े  जलए आिेिन कर सकती है और मंत्रालय क े 
साथ कई समझौता ज्ञापनों को जनष्पादित करने क े जलए पात्र होगी। तथाजप, सभी श्ेजणयों/उप-श्ेजणयों में िेय 
िार्षषक प्रोत्साहन ₹200 करोड़ प्रजत पात्र क ं पनी (समूह क ं पजनयों/िेिी सजहत) पर सीजमत होगा। 
8.5. एक ही क ं पनी क े  कई श्ेजणयों/उप-श्ेजणयों क े  जलए आिेिन करने की जस्ट्थजत में, जनिेि भी उप-श्ेजणयों क े  बीच 
अलग-अलग होंगे। 
 
9. योिना की अिजध  
9.1. इस योिना क े  अंतगषत जमलने िाला प्रोत्साहन अजधकतम पांच (05) िषष की अिजध क े  जलए प्रिान दकया 
िाएगा। योिना क े  दििा-जनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकए िाने िाले जििरणों क े आधार पर जिजभन्न क ं पजनयों 
ििारा हाजसल की गई उपलजधधयों क े  आधार पर प्रोत्साहन राजि जित्त िषष 2023-24 से 2029-30 तक िारी

दक उत्पािन प्रत्येक उत्पाि श्ेणी क े  जलए जनधाषररत पात्र सीमा से अजधक हो एिं भारत में जनर्मषत ‘जििेष 
इस्ट्पात’ क े जलए प्रजतबद्ध योग्य जनिेि सीमा की उपलजधध हो और यह पीएलआई योिना क े  अंतगषत किर हो। 
पांच (05) िषष की अिजध जित्त िषष 2022-23 (पीएलआई को जित्त िषष 2023 -24 में िारी दकया िाना है) से 
प्रारंभ होगी। तथाजप, प्रारंजभक िषष को क ु ल बिटीय आिंटन क े  भीतर जिजिष्ट उत्पाि श्ेजणयों क े  मामले में िो 
(02) िषष तक क े  जलए आस्ट्थजगत दकया िा सकता है।  
9.2. यदि आिश्यक समझा िाता हो, तो दकवहीं जिजिष्ट/प्रजतक ू ल पररजस्ट्थजतयों क े कारण पात्र क ं पजनयों को आरंजभक 
िषष को एक िषष तक आस्ट्थजगत करने की अनुमजत िेकर एक (01) िषष तक की जिस्ट्ताररत अिजध क े  भीतर 
प्रोत्साहन का लाभ उठाने की अनुमजत प्रिान की िा सकती है, लेदकन प्रोत्साहन का लाभ उठाने क े  जलए समग्र 
अिजध सजचिों क े अजधकारप्राि समूह  (ईिीओएस) क े अनुमोिन से अजधकतम पांच िषष (05) तक सीजमत

है, तो पहले िषष क े  जलए उत्पािन लक्ष्य पांच साल क े  अंत में उत्पािन लक्ष्य और अनुमाजनत सीएिीआर से 
पीछे की ओर कायष करक े प्राि दकया िाएगा। प्रोत्साहन की गणना क े  उद्देश्य से, िृजद्धिील उत्पािन आंकड़े 
आिेिक द्वारा प्रस्ट्तुत लेखा पररजक्षत िार्षषक जबरी डेटा से प्राि दकए िाएंगे और इवहें चालू िषष क े जलए 
भाररत औसत जबरी मूल्य (करों का जनिल) से जिभाजित दकया िाएगा, जिसक े जलए प्रोत्साहन का िािा 
दकया िा रहा है। इस प्रकार प्राि िृजद्धिील उत्पािन क े  आंकड़ों को चालू िषष या आधार िषष (2019 -20) क े 
जलए औसत प्रजत टन जबरी मूल्य (करों का जनिल) से गुणा दकया िाएगा और िेय प्रोत्साहन की गणना करने 
क े  जलए पीएलआई िर (िैसा लागू हो) से गुणा दकया िाएगा।  
       क = जपछले िषष या आधार िषष, िो भी अजधक हो, क े  संिभष में चालू िषष में िृजद्धिील जबरी  
       ख= चालू िषष में भाररत औसत जबरी मूल्य (करों का जनिल)  
       ग= आधार िषष (2019 -20) में भाररत औसत जबरी मूल्य  
प्रोत्साहन = (क/ख) x (ख या ग, िो भी कम हो) x (पीएलआई िर, िैसा लागू हो)/100

12. संगणना का आधार   
12.1. िृजद्धिील उत्पािन का आकलन जनर्मषत ‘जििेष इस्ट्पात’ ग्रेड क े  जबरी संबंधी आंकड़ों क े  आधार पर दकया 
िाएगा। पात्र क ं पजनयााँ जबरी क े  आंकड़े इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा सत्यापन क े अध्यधीन लेखापरीजक्षत प्रमाणपत्रों 
क े  साथ प्रस्ट्तुत करेंगी। इसक े  बाि िृजद्धिील उत्पािन उस ग्रेड क े  भाररत औसत जबरी मूल्य का उपयोग करक े 
पररकजलत दकया िाएगा।

12.2. पीएलआई की गणना की जिस्ट्तृत रूप-रेखा को योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा। 
13. योिना क े  कायाषवियन क े  जलए नोडल एिेंसी  
13.1. इस योिना क े  कायाषवियन में सहायता हेतु इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय एक एिेंसी को जनयुि कर    सकता है।  
13.2. ऐसी नोडल एिेंसी एक पररयोिना प्रबंधन एिेंसी (पीएमए) क े  रूप में कायष करेगी और सजचिीय, प्रबंधकीय 
और कायाषवियन संबंधी सहायता प्रिान करने और इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा समय-समय पर सौंपे गए अवय 
कतषव्यों को पूरा करने क े जलए जिम्मेिार होगी। पीएमए क े जिस्ट्तृत गठन, कामकाि और जिम्मेिाररयों का 
जिस्ट्तृत धयौरा योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत दकया िाएगा।  
14. अनुमोिन एिं संजितरण प्रदरया  
14.1. चयजनत क ं पजनयों की सूची इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा इस्ट्पात मंत्री क े  अनुमोिन से अजधसूजचत की िाएगी।  
14.2. एक बार अजधसूजचत होने क े उपरांत, प्रत्येक चयजनत क ं पनी योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत प्रारूप में 
योिना क े  जनबंधन और ितों का पालन करने की प्रजतबद्धता करते हुए मंत्रालय क े  साथ समझौता ज्ञापन 
(एमओयू) पर हस्ट्ताक्षर करेगी।  
14.3. पीएलआई िािा उस चयजनत क ं पनी (क ं पजनयों) द्वारा प्रस्ट्तुत दकया िाएगा जिसक े  साथ मंत्रालय ने समझौता 
ज्ञापन पर हस्ट्ताक्षर दकए हैं। पीएलआई िािा को प्रस्ट्तुत करने क े  तरीक े  योिना दििाजनिेिों में अजधसूजचत 
दकए गए अनुसार होंगे।  
14.4. योिना क े  तहत प्रोत्साहन 01-04-2022 से प्रभािी होगा और जित्तीय िषष 2023-24 में िेय होगा या िैसा 
दक योिना दििा-जनिेिों में उत्पाि श्ेणी/ उप-श्ेणी क े  जलए अजधसूजचत दकया गया हो।  
14.5. चूंदक यह योिना सीजमत जनजध िाली है; इसजलए िेय प्रोत्साहन इस योिना क े  जलए आिंरटत बिट से अजधक 
नहीं होगा। इसक े  अलािा, िेय िार्षषक प्रोत्साहन सभी उत्पाि श्ेजणयों में प्रजत पात्र क ं पनी (समूह 
क ं पजनयों/िेिी सजहत) ₹200 करोड़ तक सीजमत होगा।  
15. सजचिों का अजधकारप्राि समूह (ईिीओएस)  
15.1. मंजत्रमंडल सजचि की अध्यक्षता में सजचिों का अजधकारप्राि समूह (ईिीओएस) पीएलआई योिना की 
जनगरानी करेगा, योिना क े अंतगषत व्यय की आिजधक समीक्षा करेगा, सभी पीएलआई योिनाओं की 
एकरूपता सुजनजित करेगा और यह सुजनजित करने क े जलए उजचत कायषिाही करेगा दक व्यय जनधाषररत 
पररव्यय क े  भीतर है। 
15.2. यदि आिश्यक समझा िाता हो, तो योिना में कोई भी संिोधन इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय द्वारा ईिीओएस क े 
अनुमोिन से दकया िा सकता है।  
16. लेखापरीक्षा  
16.1. इस योिना क े  अंतगषत प्रोत्साहन का िािा करने क े  जलए समझौता ज्ञापन पर हस्ट्ताक्षर करने िाली चयजनत 
क ं पनी/क ं पजनयों का जित्तीय, कायाषत्मक और तकनीकी लेखापरीक्षा करने क े  जलए इस्ट्पात मंत्रालय को 
अजधका र दिया िाएगा। 
16.2. सांजिजधक लेखापरीक्षा जनयंत्रक एिं महालेखा परीक्षक (सीएिी) द्वारा की िाएगी।

MINISTRY OF STEEL 
NOTIFICATION 
New Delhi, the 29th July, 2021 
SUBJECT:  PRODUCTION LINKED INCENTIVE SCHEME (PLI) FOR SPECIALTY STEEL IN 
INDIA 
F. No. S-21018/1/2020-TRADE-TAX-PART(1).— 
1. INTRODUCTION: 
The Cabinet has approved the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Specialty Steel in India 
to be implemented over FY 2023-24 to FY 2029-30 with a budgetary outlay of ₹ 6,322 crore. 
2. Objective 
The objective of the PLI Scheme for specialty grade steel is to promote manufacturing of such steel 
grades within the country. Presently, India operates at the low end of the value chain for steel man u-
facturing, with an average realization per ton being ₹ 51,000-₹ 58,000 only, in contrast to our steel im-
port which have an average value per ton of about ₹ 1,46,000 - ₹ 1,83,000. PLI incentive will boost the 
domestic production of ‘Specialty Steel’ and attract significant investment for production of ‘Specialty 
Steel’ in the country. It will also help the Indian steel industry mature in terms of technology as well as 
move up the value chain. 
3. Quantum of Incentive 
The Scheme will extend production-linked incentive at the rates given in Table-1 to eligible companies.  
 
 
[भाग I—खण् ड 1] भारत का रािपत्र : असाधारण 11 
Table-1 
PLI Slab 2022-23 2023-24 2024-25 2025-26 2026-27 
PLI – A 4% 5% 5% 4% 3% 
PLI – B 8% 9% 10% 9% 7% 
PLI – C 12% 15% 15% 13% 11% 
4. Incentive Slabs 
The incentive slabs have been proposed based on the current production i.e., higher incenti ve for those 
specialty grades which are currently either not produced in India or are produced in small quantities r e-
sulting in relatively large import. Considering the average value of these specialty steel, import of these 
grades results in a substantial forex outflow from the country.  
5.    Target Segments 
5.1 The Scheme shall be applicable for five (05) indicative product categories, listed as follows: 
5.1.1 Coated/Plated Steel Products 
5.1.2 High Strength/Wear Resistant Steel  
5.1.3 Specialty Rails  
5.1.4 Alloy Steel Products and Steel Wires 
5.1.5 Electrical Steel 
An illustrative list of product sub -categories under the above five product categories, the PLI slabs 
along with eligibility criteria is enclosed as Appendix -C. These are indicative and the d etails will be 
notified in the Scheme guidelines. If considered necessary, any changes in the broad categories, sub -
categories, eligibility criteria, PLI rate etc. may be carried out as may be decided by the Ministry of 
Steel with the approval of EGoS. 
6. Eligibility 
6.1 A company registered in India under the Companies Act 2013, that is engaged in end -to-end 
manufacturing of the identified ‘Specialty Steel’ grades, subject to the input material being melted 
and poured within the country using iron ore/scrap/sponge iron/pellets etc., shall be eligible to ap-
ply for incentive under the Scheme. 
6.2 Joint ventures are also eligible to participate in the scheme. 
6.3 The net worth of the company (including that of the group companies)/JV as on date of the appl i-
cation shall not be less than 30% of the total committed investment as may be notified in the 
guidelines. 
6.4 It would be permissible to undertake a maximum 20% of the total value -addition through third 
parties (outside the group companies or JVs); the incent ive in such cases can however only be 
claimed by the company that has manufactured the end product sub-category that is eligible under 
the PLI Scheme. 
6.5 Details of the proposed minimum qualifying eligibility thresholds to be committed by the comp a-
ny will  be notified in the Scheme guidelines. An illustrative list of product category -wise e x-
pected total investment and minimum incremental production for PLI eligibility is enclosed as 
Appendix-C. 
6.6 If considered necessary, any change(s) in any eligibility c riteria may be carried out by the Mini s-
try of Steel with the approval of the Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS). 
6.7 An eligible company availing benefit under the PLI Scheme of Ministry of Steel may avail ben e-
fits under other schemes such as the Remiss ion of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products 
(RoDTEP) or those of the State Governments. 
12  THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY    [PART I—SEC.1] 
6.8  An eligible company availing benefit under the PLI Scheme of Ministry of Steel is not barred 
from availing benefits under PLI schemes of other Ministries/Departments for products other than 
those identified under the PLI Scheme for ‘Specialty Steel’. However, the eligible inves t-
ments/sales considered for benefits under this scheme shall not be considered for fiscal benefits 
under PLI schemes of other Ministries/Departments. 
7. Selection 
7.1 A transparent selection process to shortlist eligible companies/JVs registered in India under the 
Companies Act 2013 (Hereinafter referred to as eligible company/companies) shall be notified 
in the Scheme guidelines. 
7.2 Eligible companies desirous of participation in the Scheme for claiming Production -linked in-
centive can join the Scheme by applying in the prescribed format along with application fee as 
may be notified. 
7.3 Eligible companies would be required to make commitments  on parameters, namely, minimum 
year-on-year incremental production and investment which can be front -loaded or made in 
tranches. 
7.4 Applications shall be scrutinized, and selection of eligible applicants made under each of the 
five product categories/sub-categories as offered by the applicant. 
7.5 From among the eligible companies, selection in each product category/sub-category shall be on 
the basis of the following criteria –  
7.5.1 Minimum YoY incremental production commitment – Overall weightage 50% 
7.5.2 Minimum investment commitment for the product category/sub -category – Overall 
weightage 50%.  
7.5.2.1 The details of permissible investment in each product category/sub -category shall 
be notified in the Scheme guidelines.  
7.6 Investment which has been made by the eligible companies, after the date of notification of the 
Scheme, will be considered ‘eligible investment’ under the PLI Scheme subject to the condition 
that these investments are from among the list of permissible investments for the relevan t prod-
uct category/sub-category. The investment so made will count towards total investment commit-
ted to be made by the applicants during the Scheme period. Product category/sub -category-wise 
‘list of permissible investment’ shall be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
7.7 List of permissible investment would include investment in R&D which is capitalised, expend i-
ture on Transfer of Technology, payments towards Intellectual Property rights, quality control 
equipment etc., as may be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
7.8 Shortlisted eligible companies, after approval of the Minister -in-Charge, shall be notified as the 
selected companies by the Ministry of Steel. 
7.9 Selected companies under the PLI Scheme shall have to sign a Memorandum of Understanding 
with the Ministry of Steel with validity till the final year of PLI disbursal adhering to the co m-
mitments made at the time of selection. 
7.10 Performance Security which may not be less than 0.5% of the committed investment, as may be 
notified in the Scheme guide lines, shall be submitted along with the Memorandum of Unde r-
standing. 
7.11 Details of pre-qualification and selection criteria shall be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
8.  Other Conditions 
8.1 Eligible companies will have the option of front -loading investment or in tranches. In case the 
prospective manufacturer seeks a deferment in the investment schedule owing to some mitiga t-
ing circumstances such as force majeure, the same may be considered with the approval of 
[भाग I—खण् ड 1] भारत का रािपत्र : असाधारण 13 
EGoS. However, full cumulative investment will be required to be made by the eligible comp a-
nies to claim complete disbursement of PLI. 
8.2 Preference shall be given to eligible companies committing to front-load their investment during 
the scheme period. The investment commitment is proposed to be evaluated based on Net Pr e-
sent Value discounted at the rate keeping in view the Bank Rate, as may be notified in the 
Scheme guidelines. 
8.3 As the Scheme would be fund limited, in case the company fails to meet the committed thres h-
old in any given year,  it will not receive any benefits for that year. However, it will be eligible 
to receive the benefits under the scheme for the next year if it meets the cumulative committed 
thresholds defined for that year. The illustrative projected incremental production is as indicated 
in Appendix -B. The details of the projected incremental production for each product sub -
category will be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
8.4 An eligible company may apply to participate in multiple product categories/sub -categories and 
will be eligible to enter multiple MoUs with the Ministry. However, the annual incentive pay a-
ble shall be capped at ₹200 crore per eligible company (including that of group companies/JV) 
across all categories. 
8.5 In case of the same company applying for multiple categories/sub-categories, the investment 
shall also be distinct. 
9. Tenure of the Scheme 
9.1 Incentive under the Scheme shall be provided for a maximum period of five (05) years. The r e-
lease of incentive will be from FY 2023 -24 to 2029-30 based on the achievements made by dif-
ferent companies in keeping with the details to be notified in the Scheme guidelines. The ince n-
tive shall be payable to eligible companies for incremental production on a year -on-year basis, 
subject to such production being ab ove the eligible threshold prescribed for each product cat e-
gory and achievement of committed eligible investment threshold for ‘Specialty Steel’ manufac-
tured in India and covered under the PLI Scheme. The period of five (05) years will commence 
from FY 202 2-23 (PLI to be released in FY 2023 -24). The initial year may, however, be d e-
ferred by up to two (02) years in case of specific product categories within the overall budgetary 
allocation. 
9.2 If considered necessary, due to  any special/adverse circumstances, the eligible companies may 
be allowed to avail the incentive  within an extended period of up to one (01) year by allowing 
deferment of the initial year by one year  but the overall period for availing incentive being li m-
ited to a maximum period of five (05) years with the approval of Empowered Group of Secretar-
ies (EGoS). 
10. Base Year: Financial Year 2019-20 shall be treated as the base year. 
11. Incentive Outlay 
11.1 Total Incentive: The expected indicative annual incentive outlay and cumulative incentive 
outlay under the Scheme is as mentioned in Table-2.

11.2  The year -wise outlay is indicative wh ich includes the cost incurred towards Administrative 
charges. 
11.3 Incentive Per Company: Incentive per eligible company (including that of group companies/JV) 
will be applicable on incremental production of manufactured steel grades year -on-year worked 
out with reference to production in the previous year or the base year, whichever is higher, su b-
ject to an annual ceiling of ₹200 crores. In case baseline production is Nil, production target for 
the first year will be arrived at by working backwards from t he production target at the end of 
five years and the projected CAGR. For the purpose of calculating incentive, incremental pr o-
duction figures shall be derived from audited annual sales data submitted by the applicant and 
divided by the weighted average sa les price (net of taxes) for the current year for which ince n-
tive is being claimed. The incremental production figures thus derived would be multiplied by 
average per ton sales price (net of taxes) for the current year or the base year (2019 -20) which-
ever is less and multiplied by PLI rate (as applicable) to calculate the payable incentive. 
A = Incremental sales in current year with reference to previous year or the base year 
whichever is higher 
B  = Weighted Average sale price (net of taxes) in current year  
C  = Weighted Average sales price (net of taxes) in the base year (2019-20)  
Incentive = (A/B) x (B or C, whichever is lower) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100 
*Current year means the year for which PLI has been claimed. 
12. Basis of Computation  
12.1 Assessment of incremental production shall be made on the basis of the sales figures of the 
manufactured ‘Specialty Steel’ grades. The sales figures shall be submitted by the eligible 
companies along with audited certificates which will be subject to verification by the Minis-
try of Steel. The incremental production shall thereafter be worked out using the weighted 
average sales price of that grade. 
12.2 Detailed modalities of computation of PLI will be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
 
13. Nodal Agency for implementation of the Scheme 
13.1 Ministry of Steel may engage an Agency to assist in implementation of the Scheme. 
13.2 Such Nodal Agency shall act as a Project Management Agency (PMA) and be responsible 
for providing secretarial, managerial and implementation support and carrying out other r e-
sponsibilities as may be assigned by the Ministry of Steel from time to time. Detailed const i-
tution, functioning and responsibilities of the PMA will be notified in the Scheme Guid e-
lines. 
14. Approval and Disbursement Process 
14.1 The list of selected companies shall be notified by the Ministry of Steel with the approval of 
Minister of Steel. 
14.2  Once notified, each selected company shall sign a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) 
with the Ministry committing to abide by the  terms and conditions of the Scheme in the fo r-
mat as may be notified in the Scheme guidelines. 
[भाग I—खण् ड 1] भारत का रािपत्र : असाधारण 15 
14.3 PLI claim shall be submitted by the selected company(ies) with whom the Ministry has 
signed a MoU. Modalities of submission of PLI claim shall be as notifie d in the scheme 
guidelines. 
14.4 Incentive under the Scheme shall be effective from 01.04.2022, payable in FY 2023 -24 or as 
may be notified for the product category/sub-category in the Scheme guidelines. 
14.5 The Scheme is fund -limited; hence the incentive  payable shall not exceed the budgeted a l-
lotment for the Scheme. Further. the annual incentive payable shall be capped at ₹200 crore 
per eligible company (including that of group companies/JVs) across all product categories. 
15. Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS) 
15.1 The Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS) headed by the Cabinet Secretary shall monitor 
the PLI Scheme, undertake periodic review of the outgo under the Scheme, ensure uniform i-
ty of all PLI Schemes, and take appropriate action to ensure that the expenditure is within the 
prescribed outlay. 
15.2 If considered necessary, any modification(s) in the Scheme may be carried out by Ministry of 
Steel with the approval of EGoS. 
16. Audit 
16.1 Ministry of Steel shall be empowered to conduct a financial , functional, and technical audit 
of the selected company/companies who have signed MoU for claiming incentive under the 
Scheme. 
16.2 Statutory audit shall be conducted by CAG. 
RASIKA CHAUBE, Addl. Secy.
