62. What is ranking methodology?
Ranking methodology shall be guided by clause 7.4 of the guideline. An illustration of the
methodology is given below.
Illustration for Ranking methodology 
Basic Assumptions (for illustration purpose only) 
● Product Sub-category considered – Tin Mill products
● No. of Applicants - 5 (considered only for illustration purpose)
● Threshold Incremental Production Rate  eligibility as per PLI scheme (%)-  20 %
● Min. Unit Capacity =  200,000 t/yr
● Min. Unit investment = 600 Cr
Basis of assigning weightages 
Final Score (SF) =Sa x X + Sb x Y 
Where: SF=Final Score of the eligible applicant  
Sa=Normalized   Score   based   on   committed yearly incremental production data 
Sb= Normalized Score based on Committed Investment   
X=Weightage on Committed yearly production (50%)   
Y= Weightage on Committed Investment (50%) 
Sa = 100 x P/Phighest   
P = Cumulative Committed incremental production for a particular applicant   
Phighest  = Highest cumulative Committed incremental  production among all the applicants 
Sb = 100 x Q/Qhighest   
20 
15 | P a g e 1 6

Additional Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) on  
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Speciality Steel  
– Specific to PLI Scheme 1.2 
 
1 | P a g e 7  
 
1.  What are various rounds of PLI Scheme for Specialty Steel viz. PLI Scheme 1.0, PLI 
Scheme 1.1, and PLI Scheme 1.2 ? 
PLI Scheme  for specialty steel was  notified vide gazette notification on 29 th July, 
2021 , Application  window closed on Sept. 15, 2022 and  MoUs were signed on 17 
March 2023.  Subsequently, when 2 nd round of the scheme was launched in 2025, 
this round was termed as PLI scheme was termed as PLI Scheme 1.0.  
Second round of PLI Scheme for specialty steel termed as PLI Scheme 1.1 was 
launched on 06 th January 2025, application  was closed on 17th February 2025  and 
MoUs were signed on 24 th March 202 5. 
Third round of PLI Scheme for specialty steel termed as PLI Scheme 1.2  has been 
launched  on 04 th November 2025 .  
2. Where can one find details about the schemes  
One can access dedicated web portal  (https://plimos.mecon.co.in 
https://plimos.mecon.co.in/) for all the details such as scheme documents, guidelines, 
registration details as applicant, and latest notifications.  
3. Which product categories are covered under PLI Scheme 1.2? 
The are 22 Product sub -categories under  4 broad categories and detailed list can be 
found  in Annexure ‘I’  of the PLI Scheme 1.2 Guidelines.  The guidelines can be 
downloaded from web portal.

for specialty steel?

5. Who needs to give end user certificate? 
Applicants under the product sub-category 1 to 5 under product category “Steel grades for 
strategic sector” needs to provide the end user certificate for claiming incentive against 
production quantities derived from sales to be end used in strategic sector only.  
 
6. Where from the applicants need to obtain the end user certificate? 
Applicants under the product sub-category 1 to 5 under product category “Steel grades for 
strategic sector” needs to obtain the end user certificate for use in strategic sector from their 
customers whom they have sold the products under applied product sub-category of the 
scheme. 
7. Does the end user have to be direct customer? 
The end user may or may not be the direct customer. But chain of end use needs to be 
clearly established in the end user certificate. 
8. Is export quantities eligible for incentive claim ? 
Yes, for all product sub-categories. 
9. What is incentive outlay under the scheme? 
The total budget outlay of the PLI Scheme for specialty steel is Rs. 6322 Crore. 
10. What is the tenure of the scheme? 
Tenure for Production year Tenure for Incentive disbursement year 
FY 2025 – 26 to FY 2029 – 30  FY 2026 – 27 to FY 2030 – 31  
 
11. What is the mode of applying under the PLI scheme 1.2? 
A company may apply under the scheme through an online portal for the purpose. 
URL of online portal is: https://plimos.mecon.co.in

12. Is there any application fee? 
Yes, A non-refundable and one -time application fees of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST) 
for each application needs to be paid as per the prescribed mode of payment, without which 
no application shall be evaluated. 
13. What are the steps in the application process? 
Flowchart given below explains the various steps in the application process: 
 
14. Can application be submitted through offline mode? 
No, All applications are required to  be submitted through online mode only through PLI 
web portal - https://plimos.mecon.co.in.  
A hard copy of the submitted online application duly signed by the authorized signatory 
along with all supporting documents needs to be submitted at the address  given below , 
within 10 days of end date of application submission. 
The Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills,  
& Nodal Officer PLI Scheme for Specialty steel, 
MECON Limited, Vivekanand Path, Doranda, Ranchi-834002 
15.  Is applicant required to submit the signed copy of the application submitted online? 
   Yes. As explained above. 
16. Who is PMA and what is its role? 
The Scheme will be implemented through a Nodal Agency referred as Project   
Management Agency (PMA) which will be responsible for providing secretarial, 
managerial and implementation support and carrying out other responsibilities as assigned

Nodal Officer from PMA and his contact details are given below: 
Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills,

18. What is correction window? 
A onetime correction window of fifteen days (15 days) shall be notified on the PLI Portal 
after close of application submission and evaluation by PMA. It will  be available for 
completeness of supporting documents only  that were  uploaded along with the main 
application. However, no change in the main application form will be permitted. 
19. Can applicant modify the main application after submission? 
No 
20. Can a company apply for multiple product categories/ sub-categories? 
Yes, a company may apply for as many products category/ sub -category as it desires. 
However, it needs to submit separate application for each product sub -category along with 
non-refundable application fee. 
21. What is the eligibility criteria for applying under PLI Scheme 1.2? 
Please  refer to clause 4 of PLI Scheme guidelines 1.2. 
22. What is threshold incremental production rate? 
Not applicable for PLI Scheme 1.2. 
23. What is Committed rate of annual incremental production (%) year -wise over the 
entire scheme period? 
Not applicable for PLI Scheme 1.2. 
24. What Committed unit capacity  is to be installed against each applied product sub -
category?  
Refer to Annexure-I of PLI scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
25. What are permissible investments?

Refer Annexure-III of PLI scheme 1.2 Guidelines.  
26. Are investments made before the date of notification of the scheme eligible under 
permissible investment? 
No. Only the permissible investments done on or after 4th November, 2025 are admissible 
under the PLI Scheme 1.2. 
27. How is incentive calculated under PLI scheme?  
Please refer to clause 8 of the PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
28. What if an applicant company is not able to meet committed investment? 
In case the actual permissible investment made by each selected company as per Annexure-
I of PLI scheme 1.2 Guidelines, is less than the limiting investment for a product sub -
category, the selected company shall not be eligible for claiming incentive. 
29. Are taxes and duties included in the expenditure that is considered towards 
Investment in the Scheme? 
All non-creditable taxes and duties would be included in such expenditure. 
30. Is the expenditure incurred on Land and Building covered under the Scheme? 
Yes. 
31. Is there any annual ceiling on the incentive payable to each applicant?  
Refer Clause 9.1 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
32. When can an applicant submit a claim for disbursements under PLI? 
Refer Clause 9.4 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
33. What are the mandatory documents that need to be submitted along with  the 
information submitted in the application form? 
Refer appendix-A of Annexure-II of PLI scheme 1.2 Guidelines. 
34.  Is there any requirement of submitting performance security ? What is the underlying 
rationale for its submission? How much performance security is to be submitted? 
Yes, Refer Clause 5.3 of PLI Scheme 1.2 guidelines. 
35. Will the performance security be separate for each product sub-category?  
Yes.  
36. What is the basis of computation of incentive?

37.  where is clause 9.10 
There is one typographical error in the clause 9.8 wherein clause 9.10 is referred. Please 
read it as 9.9 under this clause. 
38. In case a single investment is made in single manufacturing unit to produce multiple 
sub-category products under PLI, whether one may applicant apply in multiple sub-
categories? 
Applicants applying in multiple sub -categories but proposing to use the same unit should 
comply with the individual and cumulative thresholds independently. 
Separate MoUs shall be signed by the companies for each product sub -category the 
company intends to participate.

Communication from Ministry of Steel on approval of modification of PLI Scheme 
guidelines  to be applicable for future rounds of application  
 
i. Allowing companies to commit to 50% threshold investment if they propose to create 
capacity through augmentation of existing capacities. 
ii. Reducing the thresholds for participation in high value Cold Rolled Grain-Oriented 
(CRGO) with investment threshold reduction from Rs.5000 crore to Rs.3000 crore, 
capacity threshold reduction from 200,000 tonnes to 50,000 tonnes and year-on-year 
growth threshold reduction from 40% to 10%. 
iii. Allowing carry forward of excess production to the immediately following year for the 
purpose of claiming incentive.”

Annual incremental production rate: Each applicant shall submit annual incremental 
production rate (%) year-wise along with production quantity over the scheme period 
against each applied product sub-category. The committed annual incremental production 
rate must be equal to or more than the respective threshold incremental production rate 
mentioned in Annexure-III for each product sub-category. The annual incremental
production rate shall be applicable on the committed production by applicants for each
year of the scheme tenure except first year. 
The criteria for commitment for the first year shall be as follows. - For capacity addition,
It shall be any production quantity as committed by applicant. However, in case of capacity
augmentation, the production commitment shall be more than the highest of the
production in last 3 preceding financial years i.e. FY 2021-22, FY 2022-23 & FY 2023-24 of
the applied product sub-category.
Annual Incremental production rate shall be applicable till rated capacity is achieved.

analysis.overview: The PLI Scheme for Specialty Steel is a Production Linked Incentive scheme notified by the Ministry of Steel, Government of India, to promote domestic manufacturing of specialty steel grades and enhance technology and value chain positioning of the Indian steel industry. The scheme provides financial incentives based on incremental production of eligible specialty steel products over a five-year period, with incentives disbursed from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31. It targets five broad product categories: Coated/Plated Steel Products, High Strength/Wear resistant Steel, Specialty Rails, Alloy Steel Products and Steel wires, and Electrical Steel (CRGO, CRNO).
objectives: Promote manufacturing of specialty steel grades within the country
Help the Indian steel industry mature in terms of technology
Move up the value chain in the steel industry
Incentivize incremental production of eligible specialty steel products
Support capacity addition and augmentation through new and existing facilities
analysis.eligibility: Companies registered in India under the Companies Act 2013, desirous of manufacturing identified specialty steel grades, shall be eligible to apply for incentive under the scheme. The company shall ensure end-to-end manufacturing of applied product sub-category domestically, where the input material is melted and poured within the country using iron ore/scrap/sponge iron/pellets, etc. The net worth of the company (including that of the group companies) shall not be less than 30% of the total committed investment. Third party outsourcing is allowed up to 20% of value addition. For capacity augmentation, 50% of the threshold investment shall be applicable.
analysis.benefits: Financial incentives linked to incremental production of specialty steel products, calculated as (Eligible incremental production) x (Weighted Average Sales Price in base year or current year, whichever is lower) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100. Incentive disbursement is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company per annum across all product categories. Non-financial benefits include eligibility to avail benefits under other schemes such as RoDTEP or State Government schemes, and the ability to undertake third-party value addition up to 20%.
analysis.financial_support: Incentive is calculated based on incremental production of applied product sub-category using the formula: Incentive = (A) x (B or C whichever is lowest) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100, where A = Eligible incremental production derived from sales, B = Weighted Average sales price of the applicant in current year, C = Weighted Average sales price in base year (2019-20). The PLI rate varies by slab (A, B, C) and year, ranging from 3% to 15%. The annual incentive payable is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company. Incentive disbursement begins from FY 2026-27 for the production period FY 2025-26 to FY 2029-30.
analysis.grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 200 crores per annum per eligible company
analysis.application_process: 1. Register on the PLI web portal (https://plimos.mecon.co.in). 2. Fill the application form online within 30 days from the date of launch of PLI Scheme 1.2. 3. Pay a non-refundable application fee of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST). 4. Submit the signed copy of the online application along with all uploaded documents within 10 days of online submission. 5. Undergo application scrutiny by PMA for completeness of supporting documents. 6. A one-time correction window of 15 days is available after closure of application submission for completeness of supporting documents only. 7. Selected companies sign a MoU with the Ministry of Steel. 8. Submit performance security of 0.5% of committed investment in the form of Bank Guarantee. 9. Submit undertaking consenting to audit of manufacturing facilities. 10. File annual incentive claims within 7 months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains.
required_documents: Details of source of fund (Equity and Debt)
Undertaking on value addition through third parties
analysis.deadlines: Online Registration and Application Submission dates: Start Date: 04.11.2025, End Date: To be announced (based on PLI Scheme 1.2 launch). Correction window: A one-time correction window of fifteen days (15 days) shall be notified after close of application submission and evaluation by PMA.
analysis.implementing_agency: Ministry of Steel, Government of India
analysis.contact_details: Nodal Officer from PMA: Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills, & Nodal Officer PLI Scheme for Specialty steel, MECON Limited, Vivekanand Path, Doranda, Ranchi-834002, e-mail: plischeme@meconlimited.co.in; Nodal Officer from MoS: Deputy Secretary, Steel, Ministry of Steel (MoS), Govt. of India, Udyog Bhavan, New Delhi – 110011, e-mail: pli-mosteel@gov.in
caveats: Incentive disbursement is limited to the budgeted outlay and capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company per annum.
The scheme period is for a maximum of five years, with incentive disbursement from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.
In case of deferment by up to two years for specific product categories, the incentive release will be from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31 (or FY 2027-28 to FY 2031-32 if deferred).
The actual permissible investment must be more than the limiting investment for a product sub-category to be eligible for incentive.
Performance security of 0.5% of committed investment must be submitted and is valid throughout the scheme period.
Claims for disbursement of incentive must be filed within seven months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains.
Investments made before the date of notification of the scheme (04.11.2025) are not eligible under permissible investment.
The selected company must enter into a separate MoU with the Ministry of Steel for each product sub-category applied for.
If an applicant fails to meet committed production for two consecutive years, the performance security may be invoked.
The MoS/PMA has the right to verify information and direct refund of incentives with interest in case of misrepresentation or fraud.
